subject: [PATCH v2 5/5] btrfs: cap shrink_delalloc iterations to 128M

reviews.author: Filipe Manana
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is generally good, but suggested moving a variable declaration inside the loop instead of keeping it at the top level.
reviews.raw_body:
We can also move the 'items' declaration inside this loop instead of top level.

Otherwise:

Reviewed-by: Filipe Manana <fdmanana@suse.com>

Thanks.

2026-04-06.patch_summary: This patch limits the iterations of the `shrink_delalloc` function in Btrfs to prevent it from blocking indefinitely, especially when dealing with large amounts of writeback data. The function now iterates over delalloc work in units of 128MiB, rather than trying to reclaim all available space at once. This change aims to strike a balance between making progress on delalloc and not starving other types of reservations, which can lead to system stalls and long commit times.
